free_text: New targets for on-line mass separation of nuclei
formed in 600 MeV proton and 910 MeV He-3 reactions.
Last checking has been done.
-By authors:. The production yield is defined here as
the measured intensity of the mass-separated beams in
the collector tank. The yields were determined by
in-beam beta-decay measurements at saturation for the
very short-lived products. For the long-lived products,
collection ans successive singles counting or
multiscaling of the beta activity were used in order to
determine the correct component from the complex decay
curves. The most abundant radioactive ion beams were
measured directly in a faraday cup. In order to average
short-time variation in the bombarding particle flux
and in the separation and detector efficiency, it was
found necessary to determine the intensity ratio
between two adjacent masses rather than the absolute
value. See entry o0515.
.Data-point reader uncertainty.
.By CAJAD.
Figure 1
UC2 -graphite cloth target, 10.0 g/cm**2 uranium, waso
used.
.A 4-pi plastic scintillator to count beta-
particle. It was used for mass=8.
The delayed neutrons were measured with eight
He-3 proportional counter tubes embedded symmetrically
in paraffin-wax. It was used for masses 9 and 11.
The yield of mass=7 was measured by ion
